Quick start

1. Load a site Use the bundled map or upload an Ahrefs internal links export to rebuild the visualisation in-browser.
2. Focus a page set Search for a page path or folder, then switch link direction to isolate incoming, outgoing, or two-way relationships.
3. Strip out noise Use repeated-pattern and component controls to reduce nav, footer, related-post, and card-driven links.

Preset

Blog incoming links

Applies a focused setup for reviewing links pointing into blog pages while suppressing repeated UI-driven patterns.

1 / 3

    Load Ahrefs export

    Upload an Ahrefs internal-links export or a sitemap XML file to rebuild the visualisation in this browser without running the Python script.

    Using bundled Baracuda dataset Ready for a UTF-16 Ahrefs links export.
    Importer note: Ahrefs is better for this tool because it includes real source-to-target links, anchors, noindex states, and crawl-status detail. A sitemap import is faster for URL coverage and folder structure, but it does not contain true internal-link relationships, so links, anchors, and recommendation quality will be limited.

    View guidance

    Useful when reviewing links pointing to the current matches.
    Useful when reviewing links from the current matches.
    Repeated target-and-anchor patterns stay visible but muted.
    Likely related-post, related-product, preview-card, and generic CTA links stay visible but muted.

    Most Connected Pages

    No pages match these filters.

    Link recommendations

    Suggested internal-link gaps and indexing cleanups from the current scope, prioritised for valuable content pages rather than archive or UI-driven URLs.
